chunks.content:
Article: How to Apply Evageek Knowledge to watching Eva
[]: How to Apply Evageek Knowledge to watching Eva             Quote Post by Shinji-kun » Fri Sep 25, 2009 10:03 am I can't help but feel that i have not watched Eva enough to visually take in how all anno's techniques are used/message is expressed. I keep watching other anime instead of Eva, hopefully not because i subconciously dont want to watch Eva again. Whenever I rewatched Eva in the past and even after learning from evageeks about all the deep meaning and hidden plot details, it seems like when I actually watch Eva, I am not actually analyzing/noticing/identifying these things, taking them in or learning more about Eva as i watch. Basically, how do you apply the learned concepts of Eva to the viewing of it?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Sep 25, 2009 11:43 am ...Just...watch it after reading the board for a while? What kind of answer are you expecting?

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Fri Sep 25, 2009 11:58 am Ignore the fanwanking and watch the show for yourself. :P This place is a deathtrap.

[]:             Quote Post by Rock-Slash » Fri Sep 25, 2009 12:03 pm Rub it on your back and your face, but never in the eyes.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinji-kun » Fri Sep 25, 2009 12:03 pm Lucretius wrote: Ignore the fanwanking and watch the show for yourself. :P This place is a deathtrap.

Article: How to Apply Evageek Knowledge to watching Eva
9 2:35 pm The point is, what's the point of researching Eva if you don't go back and watch it in the anime, and say, ah so that's an example of the hedgehog's dilemma being reinforced with a visual metaphor.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Fri Sep 25, 2009 2:41 pm ath wrote: master_lloyd wrote: Make a list of Anno tropes and watch the show one frame at a time?

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Fri Sep 25, 2009 2:45 pm I'd just recommend researching formalistic film study (sounds a lot more important & complex than it actually is), or if you're still in a school/college environment or have easy access to something like a community college, enrolling in a Film 101 course. Understanding the mechanics of the medium makes it easer to suck meaning out of it.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Fri Sep 25, 2009 2:47 pm master_lloyd wrote: ath wrote: master_lloyd wrote: Make a list of Anno tropes and watch the show one frame at a time?
